name: Bally Total Fitness Corporation
description: Bally Total Fitness Corporation was an American fitness club chain that operated commercial health clubs. At its peak, the company operated over 400 facilities across the United States, Canada, Mexico, and the Caribbean, serving approximately four million members. Originally founded as a subsidiary of Bally Manufacturing, the company became an independent entity and was publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker BFT. Following financial difficulties, the company underwent multiple restructurings and sold off its locations to competitors, eventually ceasing active operations.
